titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Frank Novick / Sholem Asch Collection
titlestmt.author: by Diana M. Smith

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3649773.

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[FRANK NOVICK / SHOLEM ASCH COLLECTION ])//EN" "novick.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: Paul Novick papers relating to Sholem Asch

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection consists of correspondence, writings, articles and notes by and about Sholem Asch gathered by Paul Novick, editor of the only paper willing to publish him at the height of the controversy over his trilogy, Der Man fun Notseres (The Nazarene, 1939), Der Sheliekh (The Apostle, 1943), and Meri (Mary, 1949). Jewish leaders accused Asch of teaching conversion to Christianity, a charge that Asch emphatically denied. Asch's writings reflect his concern for the Jews in Europe during and after the second World War.

langmaterial.language: Materials chiefly in Yiddish, some in English.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Charles Apfelbaum on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1992.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Frank Novick / Sholem Asch Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frank Novick / Sholem Asch Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Papers in box 3 may only be consulted with permission of the appropriate curator. Preservation photocopies for reference use have been substituted in the main files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, articles and notes by and about Sholem Asch gathered by Frank Novick, editor of the Morgen Frayhayt (Morning Freedom), a Communist Party Yiddish newspaper in New York, the only newspaper willing to publish him at the height of the controversy over his trilogy, Der Man fun Notseres (The Nazarene) (1939), Der Sheliekh (The Apostle) (1943), and Meri (Mary) (1949). Jewish leaders accused Asch of teaching conversion to Christianity, a charge that Asch emphatically denied. The correspondence between Asch and Novick make up a large part of the collection.

The Correspondence consists of letters between Asch and Novick at a time when, due to ill health, Asch was advised by his physician not to defend his controversial works in the press. These letters document Asch's reaction to the controversy. Novick's letters are represented by carbon copies. A small section of other letters, mostly to and from Novick about Asch's works, is filed at the end of the series.

The Writings consist of essays by Asch, divided into one section of complete works, and one of incomplete and untitled works. The essays are organized alphabetically by transliterated Yiddish with the English translation appearing in parentheses. The incomplete works have been summarized. The miscellaneous section contains works about Asch, probably written by Novick. Most of the articles have been placed in restricted fragile papers, and reference photocopies have been supplied. In two cases, only the article about Asch was photocopied, and the rest of the paper itself may be consulted in restricted fragile papers.

did.unittitle: Asch to Mr. Perelman of the Morning Freiheit
Committee of Jewish Writers and Artists
Between Novick and G. P. Putnam's Sons
"Eyner fun Dray Milyon" (One in Three Million) typescript, corrected
"Farlozt Aykh auf di Boyes" (Rely on the "Boys[?]) holograph, corrected
"Far Yedn Geeynigten Aden [?]--a Bombe--Daytschland" (For Every United [?] --a Bomb--Germany) holograph, corrected
"Fir Brider Zenen Mir Geven" (We Were Four Brothers) holograph, corrected
"Varshe: A fantastish motiv far a [?]" (Warsaw: A fantasy motif for [?]) holograph, corrected
"Der Yidisher Pabyedanavtsar in Zayn Senat" (The Jewish Pabyedanavtsar [?] in his Senate) holograph, corrected
"A Yidish Kind in Shanos 5695: Gevidmet di Kinder in Poylen" (A Jewish Child in the Year 1944/45) holograph, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With leaf of instructions to publisher

did.unittitle: "Zey Hobn Nisht Keyn Gloybn in Sotsializm" (They Have No Belief in Socialism)
"Zolen di Toyte Begrobn di Toyte" (Let the Dead Bury the Dead) holograph, corrected
Untitled essay beginning "Dem tog hot Got geshafn" (God created the day...)

Untitled essay beginning "Ven a ideal aroysgerufn durch noyt un umgezikhtigkayt [?] ferfelt oystsufirn zayn misie far welkhn tsvek er iz bashtimt...." (When an ideal called forth by need and lack of foresight [?] fails to accomplish its mission for which purpose it is designated....)
Untitled essay of personal reflections, holograph, corrected
Article about the English Labour Party, typescript, corrected, fragment
Article discussing his life and the Asch-Rabinowitz Collection at Yale, typescript, corrected, fragment
Condemnation of Abraham Kahn, typescript, corrected, fragment

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Papers in box 3 may only be consulted with permission of the appropriate curator. Preservation photocopies for reference use have been substituted in the main files.
